stories.headline: Baidu's AI revenue just overtook its search ads for the first time
stories.blurb: China's search giant posted only a 1% revenue drop this quarter because AI product sales grew faster than its legacy ad business shrank. It's the first time Baidu has made more from AI tools than from the search advertising that built the company.
stories.why_it_matters: A major internet company just crossed the threshold where AI became its primary business.
stories.original_headline: Baidu AI Sales Eclipse Waning Legacy Ads for the First Time

stories.headline: Google's AI coding agent just designed algorithms faster than human experts
stories.blurb: AlphaEvolve uses Gemini to write and test algorithms automatically, then keeps improving them through trial and error. Google says it's already speeding up data center operations and helping scientists discover new materials—work that normally takes specialists weeks or months.
stories.why_it_matters: AI can now write optimized code for specific problems without human experts doing the heavy lifting.
stories.original_headline: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields

stories.headline: Anthropic acquires Stainless, the company that builds SDK generators
stories.blurb: Anthropic bought Stainless, a dev tools startup that auto-generates SDKs from API specs. Stainless already builds the official SDKs for Claude, OpenAI, and a dozen other APIs. The team joins Anthropic to keep working on developer tooling.
stories.why_it_matters: The SDK layer just became part of Claude's core infrastructure instead of a vendor relationship.

try_this_prompt.title: The zombie metric
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm tracking a metric that everyone reports but nobody acts on. Describe it in two sentences below. Then tell me: what behavior would actually change if this number moved 30% in either direction? If the answer is nothing, give me one metric that would make someone pick up the phone.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before the next dashboard review or quarterly planning deck.

daily_question: If AI companies are buying the tools that build their tools, what's the last thing you'd want automated away?

working_memory.pool_quality: Strong lab announcements from Tier 1 sources (Anthropic, Meta, DeepMind, Baidu). Good mix of capability launches and market moves. Anthropic Stainless acquisition is a clear power move. Baidu revenue crossover is concrete business news. Muse Spark has ambitious hook but may lean toward product launch without clear try-it angle. Pool lacks consumer-facing tools but has solid fundamentals.
coverage_gaps: Heavy on lab releases and partnerships; light on fun consumer tools or 'try this today' angles.
No mobile-first AI features or wearable updates — missing accessible hardware hooks.
Musk/OpenAI lawsuit dominated multiple outlets but is legal drama, not capability news — pool skewed toward courtroom angle.

critique_history.reason: Muse Spark fails verification — body excerpt is a truncated blog intro with no substance to judge the actual capability or 'personal superintelligence' claim. Needs read_candidate to confirm it's not

Two Anthropic picks in a 3-story slate creates vendor overload. 'Claude for Small Business' is a bare product launch without a compelling news hook beyond 'we made connectors for SMBs' — fails the 'wo

DeepMind healthcare pick fails verification and framing tests. The body excerpt shows an April 30, 2026 date but today is May 19 — this is 19-day-old content being presented as fresh news. More critic

Strong slate with three distinct vendors (Anthropic, Baidu, DeepMind), all Tier 1 primaries, verified with full body content. Anthropic-Stainless acquisition passes 'what the hell is happening' market

decisions: Initial picks: Baidu AI revenue milestone (business), Muse Spark personal superintelligence (beginner), Anthropic acquires Stainless (cross). All Tier 1, good vendor mix (Baidu, Meta, Anthropic), passes editorial tests with concrete news hooks. Mix of market move, capability claim, and strategic acquisition.
